comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review triage (round 3 PASS, signed): fixing the one valid nit — AC5 used the non-existent `--json` flag; the real flag is `--output json` (JSON is also the default). Justification for NOT adding a dedicated end-to-end drain-execution AC (advisory E6): this is a one-key config flip that changes NO code; the enrichment-drain path (#2) is already covered by the epic's merged `tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py`, and AC5's structured assertion that the plan-review verdict carries an `overlap` key already proves the flag is wired and the overlap machinery ran (not stale-global). A drain-fire-in-session AC would over-scope a config change.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Turn on the cross-ticket overlap detector (epic only-crave-art) for THIS project by
setting `verify.overlap_enabled = true` in the tracked project config `rebar.toml`.
The feature's code shipped and is tested but ships gated OFF by default; this flips the
flag on so plan-review runs the advisory store-wide overlap step and surfaces `overlap[]`
suggestions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar.toml` `[verify]` section sets `overlap_enabled = true` with an explanatory comment.
- [ ] No OTHER config key changes: `git diff rebar.toml` shows only the added
      `overlap_enabled` line + its comment block.
- [ ] The file still parses:
      `python -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('rebar.toml','rb'))"` exits 0.
- [ ] The local build reads the flag as true (not stale-global):
      `python -c "from rebar.config import load_config; assert load_config('.').verify.overlap_enabled is True"`
      exits 0.
- [ ] The overlap step executes end-to-end, proven by a structured assertion on the verdict.
      `rebar review-plan` emits JSON by default (flag is `--output json` / `-o json`; there is
      no `--json` flag):
      `rebar review-plan ade2-8c73-e010-491d --output json | python -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin); assert 'overlap' in v, v"`
      exits 0 (an empty `overlap: []` is expected until digests are enriched — the point is the
      key is PRESENT, proving the step ran and the flag is wired, not stale-global).
- [ ] Commit references this ticket (rebar-ticket trailer) and passes `make lint`.
- [ ] Change lands on `main` through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1).

## Scope

- **`rebar.toml` only — a one-key config flip, no code change.** It does not add, remove, or
  alter any source; it activates behavior in code paths that already shipped and are already
  covered by the epic's test suite (`tests/unit/test_plan_review_overlap.py`,
  `tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py`, etc.). `rebar.toml` is the tracked, git-committed project
  config (preferred by config discovery over the git-ignored `.rebar/config.conf`), so the flag
  is persistent and shared for all contributors without per-machine env-var setup. Rollback is
  setting it back to false.

## Behavioral activation — the flag gates TWO paths (both already tested)

Flipping `overlap_enabled` on is a config-only change but it ACTIVATES two independent,
already-shipped code paths. Naming both so the behavioral consequence and its controls are explicit:

1. **Plan-review overlap step** (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`): the advisory two-stage
   retrieve-then-rerank pass that populates `overlap[]` on the verdict. **Advisory only —
   never blocks a claim or close.**
2. **Opportunistic enrichment drain** (`src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py::maybe_drain`, gated on the
   same `verify.overlap_enabled` check, invoked from the ticket write paths in
   `_store/event_append.py` and `_store/push.py`): asynchronously builds the BM25F digests
   stage-1 retrieval needs. So an ordinary `rebar` ticket write MAY now opportunistically trigger
   LLM enrichment.

**Mitigation / bounding (why path #2 is safe to enable):** the drain is best-effort and
**can never fail a write** (`maybe_drain` swallows all exceptions); it only fires when there is
soaked + lease-eligible work, is bounded by the `[tool.rebar.llm] overlap_drain*` tunables
(soak-min, lease-ttl, drain-batch, gate-budget), and — crucially — is **independently
disableable**: set `overlap_drain = "off"` under `[tool.rebar.llm]` to get plan-review overlap
WITHOUT any write-path enrichment (default is `async`; `always` runs it inline for tests/CI).
Verification of path #2's bounding/never-fails behavior is the epic's existing
`tests/unit/test_enrich_drain.py` (an end-to-end drain-fire AC is intentionally NOT added here —
this ticket changes no code, only enables already-tested paths). Rollback
(`overlap_enabled = false`) disables BOTH paths together.
compiled_state.title: Enable store-wide overlap detection in project rebar.toml
